article.h1: A universal template that bends to any brand without looking generic.
article.p: This single-file HTML system includes a dynamic hero, storytelling sections, pricing, case studies, theme engine, accessibility utilities, command palette, motion system, editable content model, reusable cards, animated backgrounds, CMS-like settings, and a configurable design token layer so the same code can power agencies, startups, products, portfolios, SaaS apps, restaurants, events, creators, and more.

div.div: Swap content, keep structure, stay distinctive.
Swap content, keep structure, stay distinctive.
Swap content, keep structure, stay distinctive.

div.p: Each module can be remixed through the configuration object without changing the system’s visual sophistication. Replace content, tweak tokens, or swap presets to fit nearly any business or product.

article.p: A reusable, animated content block with layered depth, token-driven styling, and interaction hooks for modal previews, page states, and analytics.
A reusable, animated content block with layered depth, token-driven styling, and interaction hooks for modal previews, page states, and analytics.
A reusable, animated content block with layered depth, token-driven styling, and interaction hooks for modal previews, page states, and analytics.
A reusable, animated content block with layered depth, token-driven styling, and interaction hooks for modal previews, page states, and analytics.

A reusable, animated content block with layered depth, token-driven styling, and interaction hooks for modal previews, page states, and analytics.
A reusable, animated content block with layered depth, token-driven styling, and interaction hooks for modal previews, page states, and analytics.

div.h2: Layouts that feel tailored, not templated.

div.p: Use the same engine for landing pages, corporate sites, creator hubs, app marketing pages, agency portfolios, product directories, booking platforms, education brands, and editorial sites.

article.p: Preset tuned for industry narratives, content density, and visual rhythm while remaining unmistakably custom.
Preset tuned for industry narratives, content density, and visual rhythm while remaining unmistakably custom.

div.p: Swap the copy and values to fit subscriptions, retainers, service tiers, memberships, or product bundles.

article.p: For launch-ready pages
For multi-section brands
For premium experiences

div.p: Prebuilt content cards let you turn the same structure into team profiles, testimonials, editorial posts, or service pages.

article.p: Elegant card design with meta labels, CTA hooks, and room for visuals, bios, excerpts, or product summaries.
Elegant card design with meta labels, CTA hooks, and room for visuals, bios, excerpts, or product summaries.

div.p: Fully styled details/summary blocks preserve accessibility while fitting the visual language of the site.

details.summary: Can this template work for any niche?＋
details.div: Yes. The copy, presets, tokens, layout toggles, navigation labels, and cards are all driven by editable configuration.

details.summary: Is it only a landing page?＋
details.div: No. It includes enough sections and logic to function like a mini design system in a single file.

details.summary: How do I keep it from looking generic?＋
details.div: Use the preset engine, custom content maps, visual density controls, and section toggles to create a specific narrative.

details.summary: Can I split it into components later?＋
details.div: Yes. The structure and naming are organized so you can migrate to a framework later.
Yes. It includes reveal-on-scroll, parallax tilt, command palette, theme drawer, toast notifications, and dynamic rendering.
The file is self-contained and avoids external runtime dependencies. It is suitable as a prototype or advanced starter.

article.p: The form is presentational by default and can be wired to any backend later. Inputs are styled to match the design system and support quick customization.

form.onsubmit: event.preventDefault(); window.TemplateApp.toast("Message captured in demo mode.")

textarea.placeholder: Tell us about the experience you want to build.

aside.p: Open the command palette to jump between sections, switch brand presets, toggle density, and spawn demo notifications. Open the theme drawer to preview alternate looks.

div.p: Designed as a single-file advanced starter that can become almost anything while still feeling crafted.
Agencies, SaaS, product launches, portfolios, studios, restaurants, ecommerce fronts, and editorial brands.
Themes, content maps, command palette, utility classes, interactions, and animation primitives.
Replace the content object and preset values, then connect the forms and data sources you need.

input.placeholder: Search commands, sections, themes, and actions...
